resporgs.title: Dish Network

parsed.overview: DISH Network Corporation is a public connectivity company (NASDAQ: DISH) primarily providing satellite TV services through DISH TV, streaming via Sling TV, and wireless services including a cloud-native 5G network and Boost Mobile, serving over 8 million pay-TV and Sling TV subscribers with approximately 23,000 employees.[1][2][4] Founded in 1980 and headquartered in Englewood, Colorado, it merged with EchoStar in 2024, becoming a core part of the combined entity while transitioning from legacy pay-TV toward network-first connectivity with 75%+ U.S. 5G population coverage as of early 2025.[1][2]

recent_news.title: DISH Network Merges with EchoStar
recent_news.snippet: DISH Network merged with EchoStar in 2024, blending its satellite TV operations with EchoStar's satellite technology and boosting its pivot into 5G Open RAN networks and software-defined connectivity.

parsed.affiliations: Merged with EchoStar in 2024; subsidiaries include Sling TV and Boost Mobile.[1][4]

parsed.overview: Alcazar Networks, Inc. is a nationwide VoIP and ISP provider offering comprehensive technology solutions including wholesale local origination, **toll-free origination**, DID origination, SIP termination, and LNP/LRN services, serving the US and Canada markets.[1][2][7] Conceived in 2003 by ISP veterans, it operates as a RespOrg (code: ADN01) with direct access to the SOMOS Toll Free National Database and is headquartered in Trexlertown, PA, United States.[1][2][4][7] The company appears to be privately held with no disclosed parent or holding company and limited public information on employee count or revenue.[1][5]

recent_news.title: FTC Takes Action against Second VoIP Service Provider for Facilitating Illegal Telemarketing Robocalls
recent_news.snippet: The FTC settled charges against Alcazar Networks Inc. and owner Gavin Grabias for facilitating tens of millions of illegal telemarketing robocalls, including those to Do Not Call Registry numbers and with spoofed caller IDs like '911.' The settlement imposes a monetary penalty, bars future misconduct, and requires customer screening.

resporgs.title: iconectiv

parsed.overview: iconectiv, LLC is a telecommunications and IT service management company providing numbering solutions, network management, fraud prevention, and industry registries, including serving as the Local Number Portability Administrator (LNPA) for the U.S. NPAC, Secure Telephone Identity Policy Administrator (STI-PA), and TRS Numbering Directory provider.[1][3][5] Founded in 1983 as the Central Services Organization (originally Bellcore in 1984, Telcordia in 1999, renamed iconectiv in 2013), it is headquartered in Bridgewater, New Jersey, and was sold to Koch Equity Development LLC in 2025 after prior investment by Francisco Partners.[1][2][6] The private company serves over 1,200 customers globally, enabling interconnection for more than two billion people daily.[2][4]

parsed.overview: Shaw Business is the enterprise division of the former Shaw Communications, a Canadian telecommunications company providing internet, phone, TV, and data services primarily in Western Canada. Shaw Communications was founded in 1966 in Edmonton, Alberta, with headquarters in Calgary, AB, and was acquired by Rogers Communications in April 2023, after which Shaw Business operations are being integrated into Rogers Business. The company was privately held prior to acquisition and served approximately 3.2 million customers, including over 1 million business and home phone subscribers, via a 535,000-mile fiber network.[1][2][4]

recent_news.snippet: Following the 2023 acquisition, most Shaw operations were rebranded to Rogers beginning in July, using transitional 'Rogers together with Shaw' branding in former Shaw markets.

parsed.overview: Vonage Holdings Corp. is a cloud communications provider offering VoIP, unified communications, contact center solutions, and communications APIs, primarily focused on businesses following its evolution from residential services.[1][2] It operates as a subsidiary of Ericsson, which acquired it in July 2022 for $6.2 billion; founded in 1998 as Min-X and renamed Vonage in 2001, it is headquartered in Holmdel, New Jersey, United States, and was public until the acquisition (previously went public in 2006) with reported 2020 revenues of $1.25 billion.[1]

recent_news.title: Ericsson completes acquisition of Vonage
recent_news.snippet: Ericsson completed its $6.2 billion acquisition of Vonage Holdings Corp., integrating its cloud communications capabilities into Ericsson's portfolio.

parsed.affiliations: Subsidiary of Ericsson.

resporgs.title: SoVerNet

parsed.overview: SoVerNet (SoverNet Communications) was a telecommunications provider offering residential and business telephone, internet, high-capacity network transport, and voice services over a 4,500-mile fiber network in northern New England and New York, founded in 1995 with headquarters originally in Bellows Falls, Vermont.[1][3][4] In 2016, it was sold by ATN International to Oak Hill Capital Partners and merged with FirstLight Fiber and Oxford Networks to form a larger fiber-optic enterprise operating 9,500 miles of network across New York and New England.[2] It now operates as part of **FirstLight**, a private company providing scalable telecom services including private line, high-speed internet, data centers, and voice to retail and wholesale customers.[3][5]

recent_news.title: Internet service provider Sovernet will be sold
recent_news.snippet: Vermont telecommunications service provider Sovernet announced it was being sold to a private equity firm controlled by Texas billionaire Robert Bass and will combine with FirstLight Fiber and Oxford Networks.

parsed.affiliations: Merged into **FirstLight** (website: https://www.firstlight.net/) by Oak Hill Capital Partners in 2016; previously owned by ATN International.[2][3]

resporgs.title: Verizon Business

parsed.overview: Verizon Business is the enterprise division of Verizon Communications Inc., providing networking, cloud, data center, and telecommunications services to business and government clients.[1][2] Verizon Communications, its public parent company (NYSE: VZ), was formed in 2000 through the merger of Bell Atlantic and GTE, with headquarters in New York City and approximately 99,600 employees; Verizon Business operations are based in Basking Ridge, New Jersey.[2][3][4] The company reported $138.2 billion in revenues for 2025 and serves nearly all Fortune 500 companies worldwide.[2]

recent_news.snippet: Verizon achieved first positive 1Q postpaid phone net additions since 2013, with a year-over-year improvement of over 340,000, and raised adjusted EPS guidance to the top half of 750,000 - 1M range.

recent_news.title: Verizon Fact Sheet Highlights 2025 Revenues and Connections
recent_news.snippet: Verizon generated revenues of $138.2 billion in 2025, with 146.8 million total wireless retail connections as of March 31, 2026, and over 90% of planned sites covered by C-Band.

recent_news.title: Verizon Mobile Network Reaches 146.1 Million Subscribers
recent_news.snippet: Verizon is the largest wireless carrier in the United States with 146.1 million subscribers as of June 30, 2025, making it the world's second-largest telecommunications company by revenue.

resporgs.title: Somos Help Desk

parsed.overview: Somos Help Desk is the 24/7 customer support service provided by **Somos, Inc.**, a private corporation specializing in telecommunications registry management, including administration of the North American Numbering Plan (NANP) and the Toll-Free Numbering (TFN) registry as the designated RespOrg administrator (code: BRSAC).[1][2][5] Somos, Inc., formerly SMS/800 Inc., is headquartered at One Tower Center Blvd, Suite 800, East Brunswick, NJ 08816, with additional offices in Herndon, VA, Westford, MA, and Littleton, CO (near Denver); it was founded prior to 2019 when it assumed NANPA duties from NeuStar.[1][5] The company manages over 7 billion phone numbers across North America and serves RespOrgs via web portals, training, and support.[3][4][5]

recent_news.title: Somos Revolutionizes How Phone Number Assets are Managed and Protected with RealBrand
recent_news.snippet: Somos launched RealBrand, a new solution to enhance management and protection of phone number assets, providing advanced tools for brand protection in telecommunications.

parsed.toll_free_specific: Somos, Inc. (RespOrg code BRSAC) administers the SMS/800 database for RespOrgs and toll-free number assignments in the NANP, as designated by the FCC; the Help Desk supports RespOrgs with TFNRegistry services 24/7 via phone (844.439.7666), text, chat, and email.[2][5]

resporgs.title: Flowroute

parsed.overview: Flowroute is a software-centric cloud communications platform providing SIP trunking, VoIP, messaging (SMS/MMS), and CPaaS services via APIs and its patented HyperNetwork for reliable carrier-grade voice and texting.[5][6][8] It was acquired by Intrado (formerly West Corporation, a private company owned by Apollo Global Management) prior to 2022 and sold to BCM One, a private provider of NextGen communications and managed services, in March 2022; Flowroute operates from headquarters in New York, NY (previously associated with Seattle, WA).[1][2][6] Year founded is not specified in available sources; rough size includes approximately 883 employees and $48.6 million revenue as of recent data.[6]

recent_news.title: Intrado to Divest Flowroute Business
recent_news.snippet: Intrado Corporation announced it has sold the assets of the Flowroute business to BCM One, a leading provider of NextGen Communications and Managed Services. Flowroute is a cloud-based communications platform for provisioning voice and messaging services.

recent_news.title: BCM One Acquires Flowroute from Intrado Corporation
recent_news.snippet: BCM One announced the acquisition of Flowroute assets from Intrado, enhancing its CPaaS offerings with Flowroute’s HyperNetwork, advanced APIs, and automation for voice and messaging.

recent_news.title: BCM One acquires the assets of Flowroute from Intrado Corporation
recent_news.snippet: BCM One acquired Flowroute assets last month, integrating its scalable cloud communications, HyperNetwork, APIs, and messaging into BCM One’s CPaaS platform.

parsed.affiliations: Parent company: BCM One (acquired assets from Intrado Corporation in March 2022).

resporgs.title: iconectiv

parsed.overview: iconectiv, LLC is a telecommunications and IT service management company providing numbering solutions, network management, fraud prevention, and registry services, including as the U.S. Local Number Portability Administrator (LNPA) managing the NPAC for approximately 1 billion telephone numbers.[1][3][5] Originally founded in 1983 as Bell Communications Research (Bellcore), it was acquired by Ericsson in 2012, rebranded as iconectiv in 2013, and sold to Koch Equity Development LLC in 2025; it is privately held and headquartered in Bridgewater, New Jersey, serving over 1,200 customers globally.[1][2][6]

parsed.overview: Comcast Corporation is a public media and technology company primarily providing cable television, internet, phone services, and owning NBCUniversal for content and theme parks. Founded in 1963 and headquartered in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, it operates as the largest home internet service provider and second-largest cable TV company in the US, serving 40 states and the District of Columbia with approximately 182,000 employees.[1][2][3]
